title: Optimize Reloop for better array detection
body:
In SweRV C++ output the branch predictor results in code which basically makes a pattern, that if we **hand** expand to macros is this:

```
#define F(ai, bm, co, e, f, g) \
    vlTOPp->bht_bank_wr_data[ai] \
        = ((bm & vlTOPp->bht_bank_wr_data[ai]) \
           | (co & (((we2 & (e == bh0)) ? br0sh : ((we1 & (f == bh1)) ? br1sh : (exh4))) << g)))

    for (int ii=0; ii<8; ++ii) {   // In reality, is unrolled
        for (int bit=0; bit<16; ++bit) {   // In reality is unrolled
            F(ii, ~(3U<<(bit*2)), 3U<<(bit*2), bit, bit, bit*2);
```

* If common expressions are pulled out of the loop, the code is 47% faster and similar sized.
* If the already-unrolled loop for the ii index is converted to a for loop the code after above is 9% faster and 80% smaller.
* If the bits are looped the code is 40% slower and 80% smaller again - classic space/time tradeoff. Note the bit part of this loop is described in bug #2630, if that is fixed this loop optimization would not apply.

V3Reloop could be improved to look at all the I/O and constants in each statement and look for better looping opportunities.

This is likely 5% or more overall gain.

        for (int bit=0; bit<16; ++bit) {   // In reality is unrolled
            F(ii, ~(3U<<(bit*2)), 3U<<(bit*2), bit, bit, bit*2);
